page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. This server features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests fall into the average range, with scores of 1707.38 and 6816.76 respectively. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.07 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.47 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance exhibits strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 14.26 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25 percent. However, it shows weaker results in Passmark CPU tests and poor performance in the Passmark encryption test. LLM inference speeds are average to weak, with text generation for a small 135M model reaching 93.43 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space, making it suitable for applications requiring fast temporary storage. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 10.06 GB/sec, Redis key-value stores processing 9.34 million SET operations per second, software compilation, and general-purpose database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most compute and memory tasks, with a strong showing in single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, it exhibits weaker performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests. The instance delivers solid throughput for static web serving at 10.06 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 9.34 million operations per second. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient balance of dedicated ARM compute and local high-speed storage, making it suitable for web serving, caching layers, software compilation, and general-purpose database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced compute and local storage performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, balanced memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance alongside average memory bandwidth
Suitable for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose database workloads
